Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Return format:

Node started: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S3-ahc024-topological-map
Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Problem Reformulation: Topological Grid Embedding Optimization 1. Input Definition Let G = (V, E) be a planar graph representing a colored map, where: V is a set of vertices, each assigned a color c(v) in C. E is a set of edges representing adjacency between regions. Connectivity is defined by the existence of paths within monochromatic components. 2. State Space Let S be a mapping function f: V to G, where G is a 50 x 50 discrete integer grid. Each cell g in G can be in one of the following states: s(g) in {Empty} union C. 3. Constraints An embedding f is valid if and only if it satisfies the following: Color Consistency: for all v in V, s(f(v)) = c(v). Adjacency Preservation: If (u, v) in E and c(u) is not equal to c(v), then there must exist at least one pair of cells (gu, gv) such that s(gu) = c(u), s(gv) = c(v), and gu, gv are 4-neighbors (orthogonal adjacency). Connectivity Preservation: For every monochromatic subset Vi subset of V where c(v) = i for all v in Vi, the set of cells {g in G | s(g) = i} must form a single 4-connected component in G. Injectivity/Non-Overlapping: No two distinct vertices u, v can map to the same cell unless they belong to the same monochromatic component and are part of a continuous spatial block. 4. Objective Function Maximize the cardinality of the set of empty cells: Maximize Z = |{g in G | s(g) = Empty}| 5. Primary Tension The optimization requires minimizing the total number of occupied cells (minimizing the footprint of the embedding) while satisfying the topological requirement that all boundary adjacencies between distinct color sets and all internal connectivity within color sets are maintained within the 50 x 50 boundary.
